$description: Tier 2 — decision tokens (constitution §22.2): the theme-INVARIANT roles. Theme-varying roles (surface/cell/ink families) live in themes/*.tokens.json under the same names. Every color here is a literal ONLY until the engineered palette lands in primitives — then each becomes an alias, and a literal color in this file becomes a defect. Colors are structured oklch per DTCG.

stage.$description: Theme-independent asset surround (§1). User-adjustable within [min, max]; contracts must hold across the whole range, not just the default (§23).

alx.role: range cap: below the dead band so stage ink stays legible at every setting (§1)

focus ring, drop-target fill, toggles-on, links (§5). Ratified 2026-07-13, closing §30.1: the accent is USER-SELECTABLE from the named scales at runtime (blue is the default binding) — accent stopped being a hue choice and became a role bound to a user-picked scale, like attention (§29). Eligibility is gated: every offered hue must pass ring contrast on all four themes (contracts.json ring); gray is excluded (registries.json). Sharing a hue with a label is lawful — accent and labels are style-disjoint (ring/link/toggle vs solid swatch), ratified same date.
default 'needs a human' hue (§10); user-picked from the tag palette at runtime (§29). Ratified 2026-07-13: magenta — 40° from both purple and red, passes the §23 hue-distance floor.
invalid input only (§25); independent of the attention choice. Unified onto the red scale 2026-07-13 — contexts (field hairline vs swatch) never collide.

label.$description: LrC-compatible color labels (§5): the solids of the named scales (unified 2026-07-13 — LrC compatibility is the five NAMES, not exact hues). Solid swatch style only; user-toggleable off. 'yellow' is the amber scale.

fun.$description: The fun layer (§17): an injected add-on at registered sites (see registries.json), never core chrome. Recipe values are §30.8 PIN.

alx.pin: hypothesis; becomes palette-indexed stops when the named hue scales land (§17)

alx.role: degrees; gradient geometry (DTCG gradients are angle-free stop lists)
hero label on the polychrome face

face-inset.$description: Frosted face inset highlights; both layers inset (house extension flag — DTCG shadows have no inset field).

type-role.$description: The §30.4 role ladder (committed 2026-07-13): concept → typography composite; the paired ink lives in $extensions.alx.ink. Two sizes do the work (12 default, 11 dense); weight + ink + mono make the hierarchy (§9); 13 exists only for dialog titles; 10 is the micro voice. disabled is NOT a role — it is the §4 unary operator (one ink step down on whatever role). VERIFIED with real Geist (library/type-probe.html, 2026-07-13): the matched pair holds — mono needs NO size compensation (baselines and x-heights align at same px; earlier 'mono reads larger' was a fallback-font artifact); head weight = semibold 600 (700 reads shouty over dense rows). Remaining §28 check: Windows ClearType at 1×.

alx.role: view-level landmark (ratified 2026-07-13): whole-view choices like the module picker. Both display rungs are in the system deliberately — exact assignments stay open until real views compose them.
view-level page headings (Settings/Import screens) — between title and display
dialog titles only
section heads: bold at body size (§9)
buttons, menu items, tabs
content: names, editable values
the data voice: filenames, EXIF, counts (§9)
row labels, form labels

labels inside row-text dense sections — pairs with data-sm. Remapped ink.2→ink.3 (2026-07-13): in the instrument voice the values speak and labels support; the 'C' preference from the probe A/B/C was literally the muted rung. Scoped here so regular `label` (form rows) keeps ink.2 — a role remap, never a ramp move.
cell captions, hints, meta
dense data: tree counts, indexes
absent values (ratified 2026-07-13: ink.4, two full steps below the label — voice adjacency was the smell); disabled stays distinct via drained field fill
the whisper: cell indexes, keyboard hints (non-essential only, §28 check pending)

radius.$description: Radius by detachment (§24): docked = 0 (seams) → controls small → transients large. Three working values + the pill.

alx.role: re-pinned 4→6 (material round 2026-07-22 eye-gate): the reference control/pill radius.
re-pinned 8→12 (material round 2026-07-22): the reference transient/overlay radius, rounder than docked controls (§24 detachment).

z.$description: The z-order registry (§24). There is no other elevation.

alx.role: scroll-linked, cast inward onto clipped content only (§6)

size.$description: Chrome sizing quanta (§8): two control heights, one icon size, uniform row rhythm. All §30.5 PIN.

alx.role: THE default control height (ratified 2026-07-13, was control-sm): buttons, fields, toolbar chrome — the dense-tool default; = the hit-target floor. A 24 control inside a 28 row-control is the canonical field-row rhythm.
prominent controls only (was control-md, demoted from default 2026-07-13): dialog CTAs, hero spots
rows carrying interactive controls (fields, buttons) — controls breathe (ratified 2026-07-13)
interactive dense rows (source tree, list selections) — sits ON the hit-target floor; reuses the control-sm/hit-target quantum

read-only data rows (metadata, EXIF) — the instrument voice, ratified 2026-07-13: 16px row PAIRED with the small type roles (label-sm/data-sm; body type in a 16px row is zero-slack and banned — see registries.json rowIntents). Density is vertical only: insets stay at row-inset (§8 horizontal generosity). Non-interactive only. One line unit: lh 16 = row height.
identical generous side insets; text never touches an edge (§8)

alx.pin: hypothesis (§30.9) — in tension with the §8 1× floor; empirical check pending

alx.role: interior cell padding — photos never touch (§19)

registered sub-grid exception (material round 2026-07-22): the grouped-control track inset at the dense control height. 2px is below the 4px quantum by design — a full quantum crops a 24px control's segment to 16px (cramped); 2px keeps a 20px segment. control-lg affords the full space-1.
1px inner containment border on thumbnails (§19)
